steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The 40 students were randomly selected from fourth-graders at one specific school.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Random samples only generalize to the population from which they were drawn.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Since the sample came from fourth-graders at that school, results apply to all fourth-graders at that school — not to other grade levels or other schools.

eliminationHtml:
• Choice A — The 40 students who were surveyed: This is just the sample itself, not a broader population. ✗
  • Choice B — All fourth-grade students at the school: This is exactly the population the random sample was drawn from. ✓
  • Choice C — All students at the school: Includes grade levels not represented in the sample, so results cannot be extended there. ✗
  • Choice D — All fourth-grade students in the county in which the school is located: Students from other schools were never sampled, so the results cannot generalize to them. ✗
That matches Choice B — All fourth-grade students at the school.
